# UC-17.1.66 · Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics — IoT/OT Classification Confidence and New-Profile Discovery

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE uses AI to identify what kind of device just connected. We watch when the AI is unsure or when it sees a brand-new type of device, so we can teach it and refine the rules.*

---

## Description

Tracks Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics classification confidence and discoveries of new endpoint types — useful for IoT/OT environments where unknown devices appear and need classification before policy assignment.

## Value

AI Endpoint Analytics complements traditional Profiler with ML-based classification. Tracking new-type discoveries provides input to policy refinement; tracking low confidence catches systematic gaps.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Connect a never-seen IoT device. AIEA emits 80101 (new type). UC-17.1.66 fires.

### Negative scenario

Connect a well-known device type. High confidence; no fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80100, 80101, 80102)
| stats count, dc(endpoint_mac) as endpoints, avg(confidence_score) as avg_conf by endpoint_type, MessageCode
| where avg_conf < 70 OR MessageCode=="80101"
```

## Visualization

Confidence distribution; new-discovery trend.

## Known False Positives

- Initial deployment — many low-confidence classifications during model warm-up.

## References

- [Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
